blueprint.title: Acme Apparel AI Pilot: Store-Level Inventory Replenishment & Shrink Detection Across 40 Locations

personas.name: District Inventory Planner at Acme Apparel (40-store specialty chain)
personas.description: Responsible for allocating and balancing stock across all 40 store locations, managing inter-store transfers, and ensuring SKU availability aligns with regional demand patterns.
goals: Minimize lost sales due to stockouts at high-velocity stores
Reduce overstock at underperforming locations through proactive rebalancing
Automate inter-store transfer recommendations to cut manual planning time
pain_points: Manually reviewing sell-through reports across 40 stores is time-consuming and error-prone
Transfer requests often arrive too late, after a SKU has already sold through
No systematic way to identify which donor store has the right size/color depth to fulfill a transfer

personas.name: Store Operations Manager at Acme Apparel (individual store, high-volume urban location)
personas.description: Oversees daily store operations including receiving, floor replenishment, cycle counts, and POS reconciliation for a single high-traffic Acme Apparel location.
goals: Keep floor inventory accurate and fully stocked during peak selling periods
Identify and escalate shrink incidents before they compound into significant loss
Reduce time spent on manual cycle count reconciliation
pain_points: Shrink is often discovered weeks after the fact during full physical inventory counts
POS voids and returns anomalies are not flagged in real time
Cycle count discrepancies are logged in spreadsheets and rarely escalated systematically

personas.name: Loss Prevention Analyst at Acme Apparel (regional, covering 12 stores)
personas.description: Monitors shrink trends, investigates POS anomalies, and coordinates with store managers and LP field agents to identify and resolve theft or process-failure incidents across a regional cluster of stores.
goals: Detect shrink patterns early using POS transaction data and cycle count variances
Prioritize which stores and SKU categories warrant on-site investigation
Build evidence-based cases for internal investigations or vendor disputes
pain_points: Shrink data from POS and cycle counts lives in separate systems with no unified view
High volume of transaction data makes manual pattern detection impractical
Alerts from store managers are inconsistent and often lack supporting data

scenarios.title: Real-Time SKU Sell-Through Detection and Automated Inter-Store Replenishment
scenarios.description: A best-selling slim-fit denim SKU in size 32x30 sells through at a downtown flagship store on a Friday afternoon. The AI system detects the stockout risk, identifies donor stores with surplus inventory, calculates optimal transfer quantities, and routes a transfer recommendation to the District Inventory Planner for approval before end of business day.
steps: POS Transaction Monitor Agent detects that on-hand units for SKU #DM-3230-IND have dropped below the store-specific reorder threshold at the flagship location, cross-referencing real-time POS sales velocity against current on-hand inventory from the WMS feed.

Inventory Rebalancing Agent queries all 39 other store locations for available depth in SKU #DM-3230-IND, filtering for stores within a 50-mile radius with on-hand units exceeding their own 14-day demand forecast by at least 3 units.
Inventory Rebalancing Agent generates a ranked list of 3 donor store candidates with proposed transfer quantities, estimated transit time, and projected impact on donor store coverage days.

District Inventory Planner (p1) receives a structured transfer recommendation in the planning dashboard, reviews the donor store options, and approves the optimal transfer with a single confirmation action.
Store Operations Manager (p2) at the flagship receives an inbound transfer notification with expected arrival window and is prompted to confirm floor space readiness.
Transfer order is automatically submitted to the logistics coordination system and a confirmation is logged in the inventory audit trail.

scenarios.title: Early Shrink Pattern Detection from POS Anomalies and Cycle Count Variances
scenarios.description: Over a two-week period, a suburban Acme Apparel store shows a recurring pattern of post-close void transactions on premium outerwear SKUs combined with cycle count shortages in the same category. The AI system correlates these signals, scores the shrink risk, and surfaces a prioritized alert to the regional Loss Prevention Analyst with supporting evidence.
steps: POS Anomaly Detection Agent continuously ingests transaction logs from all 40 stores and flags a cluster of post-close void transactions on outerwear SKUs at store #14, noting that 7 of 9 voids over 14 days were processed by the same employee ID during closing shifts.

Cycle Count Reconciliation Agent ingests the latest cycle count submission from store #14 and identifies a variance of -11 units across 4 premium outerwear SKUs, which exceeds the store's historical variance baseline by 340%.

Shrink Risk Scoring Agent correlates the POS void pattern with the cycle count variance, assigns a high-risk shrink score of 87/100 to store #14, and tags the incident with category, SKU list, employee ID pattern, and time-of-day clustering.

Loss Prevention Analyst (p3) receives a prioritized alert in the LP dashboard with a pre-built evidence summary including transaction logs, cycle count delta report, and a timeline visualization of the anomaly pattern.

Store Operations Manager (p2) at store #14 receives a separate, role-appropriate notification instructing them to secure the outerwear section and prepare for an unannounced cycle recount, without disclosing the employee-level investigation details.
Loss Prevention Analyst (p3) reviews the evidence package, escalates to an on-site field investigation, and logs the case in the LP case management system with all AI-generated artifacts attached.

agents.name: POS Transaction Monitor Agent
agents.role: Real-time point-of-sale data ingestion and sell-through threshold alerting
responsibilities: Continuously ingest POS transaction streams from all 40 store locations
Calculate rolling sell-through velocity per SKU per store using configurable time windows
Trigger stockout risk alerts when on-hand inventory crosses store-specific reorder thresholds
Flag post-close, high-value, and sequential void transactions as anomaly candidates
inputs: Real-time POS transaction feed (sales, returns, voids) from all 40 stores
Current on-hand inventory levels from WMS integration
Store-specific reorder threshold configuration per SKU category
Store operating hours and shift schedule data
outputs: Stockout risk alerts with SKU, store ID, current on-hand, and velocity metrics
Void transaction anomaly flags with employee ID, time, SKU, and transaction value
Sell-through velocity reports per SKU per store for downstream agents

agents.name: Inventory Rebalancing Agent
agents.role: Cross-store inventory analysis and inter-store transfer recommendation generation
responsibilities: Query all store locations for available inventory depth on flagged SKUs
Apply demand forecasting logic to identify true surplus at potential donor stores
Rank donor store candidates by transfer feasibility, proximity, and coverage impact
Generate structured transfer recommendations with quantity, donor store, and logistics details
inputs: Stockout risk alerts from POS Transaction Monitor Agent
On-hand inventory levels for all 40 stores from WMS
14-day demand forecast per SKU per store from planning system
Store geolocation data and inter-store logistics cost matrix
outputs: Ranked donor store candidate list with proposed transfer quantities
Coverage day impact analysis for both receiving and donor stores
Draft transfer orders ready for planner approval
Inventory rebalancing audit log entries

agents.name: Cycle Count Reconciliation Agent
agents.role: Automated cycle count variance detection and baseline deviation scoring
responsibilities: Ingest cycle count submissions from all store locations as they are completed
Compare counted quantities against system-of-record on-hand balances
Calculate variance magnitude and compare against each store's historical variance baseline
Tag high-deviation SKUs and categories for shrink risk correlation
inputs: Cycle count submission files from store operations teams
System-of-record on-hand inventory balances from WMS
Historical cycle count variance baselines per store and per SKU category
Store cycle count schedule and completion status
outputs: Cycle count variance reports per store with SKU-level detail
Baseline deviation scores indicating statistical significance of variances
High-variance SKU and category flags passed to Shrink Risk Scoring Agent
Cycle count completion and accuracy trend reports for store operations managers

agents.name: POS Anomaly Detection Agent
agents.role: Pattern recognition across POS transaction data to surface potential shrink-related behaviors
responsibilities: Apply rule-based and statistical models to detect anomalous POS transaction patterns
Identify clustering of voids, refunds, or no-sale events by employee, time, and SKU
Correlate transaction anomalies across multiple days to distinguish noise from patterns
Enrich anomaly flags with contextual metadata for downstream investigation
inputs: Historical and real-time POS transaction logs including voids, returns, and no-sales
Employee shift schedule and ID mapping data
SKU price tier and category data
Store-level transaction volume baselines for normalization
outputs: Anomaly event records with employee ID, transaction type, SKU, time, and store
Pattern severity scores based on frequency, recency, and value concentration
Structured anomaly summaries passed to Shrink Risk Scoring Agent
Raw anomaly logs for LP case management attachment

agents.name: Shrink Risk Scoring Agent
agents.role: Multi-signal shrink risk correlation and prioritized alert generation for loss prevention
responsibilities: Correlate POS anomaly patterns with cycle count variances at the store and SKU level
Compute a composite shrink risk score using weighted signal inputs
Classify incidents by likely shrink type (internal theft, external theft, process failure, vendor shortage)
Generate prioritized, evidence-backed alerts for the Loss Prevention Analyst
inputs: Anomaly event records and pattern severity scores from POS Anomaly Detection Agent
High-variance SKU flags and deviation scores from Cycle Count Reconciliation Agent
Historical shrink incident outcomes for model calibration
Store staffing and security configuration metadata
outputs: Composite shrink risk scores per store with contributing signal breakdown
Prioritized LP alert queue with evidence summaries and recommended actions
Incident classification tags for case management routing
Trend reports on shrink risk by store cluster, SKU category, and time period

agents.name: Planner and LP Notification Orchestration Agent
agents.role: Role-appropriate alert routing, approval workflow management, and audit trail maintenance
responsibilities: Route inventory transfer recommendations to the correct District Inventory Planner based on store cluster assignment
Deliver role-scoped shrink alerts to Loss Prevention Analysts and Store Operations Managers without exposing sensitive investigation details to store-level recipients
Manage approval state for transfer orders and escalate unactioned recommendations after configurable SLA windows
Maintain a complete audit log of all agent-generated recommendations, approvals, and outcomes
inputs: Transfer recommendations from Inventory Rebalancing Agent
Prioritized LP alerts from Shrink Risk Scoring Agent
User role and store cluster assignment directory
Approval decisions and acknowledgment events from dashboard users
outputs: Routed notifications delivered to planner and LP dashboards and mobile alerts
Approved transfer orders submitted to logistics coordination system
Escalation notices for unactioned recommendations past SLA threshold
Complete recommendation-to-outcome audit trail for compliance and model feedback
